nodes.label: Greetings & Set Phrases
nodes.description: Master essential daily greetings, introductions, and leaving/returning phrases.
nodes.cheatsheet: Ask names: **Jak masz na imię?** (*What is your name?*). Leaving/returning patterns: **Wychodzę** (*I'm leaving*) or **Do zobaczenia** (*See you later*). When returning or greeting at home: **Już jestem** (*I'm back*) and **Witaj w domu** (*Welcome home*).

nodes.label: Countries & Identity
nodes.description: Talk about nationalities, languages, and core family relationships.
nodes.cheatsheet: Nationalities change by gender: **Jestem Polakiem** (m) / **Polką** (f). Languages use **po** + adverb: **Mówię po polsku** (*I speak Polish*). For 'Mom', use the warm **mama** or formal **matka**.

nodes.label: Numbers, Hundreds & Thousands
nodes.description: Count higher figures into hundreds and thousands, and learn basic fractions.
nodes.cheatsheet: Hundreds: **sto** (100), **dwieście** (200), **trzysta** (300). Thousands: **tysiąc** (1000), **dwa tysiące** (2000). Fractions use **dwie trzecie** for 2/3 (nominative feminine form for parts).

nodes.label: Demonstratives (This, That, Which)
nodes.description: Identify objects using gendered proximity pronouns.
nodes.cheatsheet: Demonstratives must match the noun's gender: **ten** (m), **ta** (f), **to** (n) for 'this'. For 'that': **tamten**, **tamta**, **tamto**. 'Which' is **który** / **która** / **które**.

nodes.label: Colours & Descriptions
nodes.description: Use basic color vocabulary matching noun genders.
nodes.cheatsheet: Colors act as adjectives and agree in gender: **czerwony/czerwona/czerwone** (red), **niebieski** (blue), **czarny** (black), **biały** (white), **zielony** (green).

nodes.label: General Counters
nodes.description: Count structural physical objects and learn counting shifts.
nodes.cheatsheet: Counting changes forms: 1 is **jeden/jedna/jedno**. 2, 3, 4 take nominative plural (**dwa domy**), while 5+ triggers genitive plural (**pięć domów**). Ask 'How many?' with **Ile?**.

nodes.label: Floors & Negative Numbers
nodes.description: Navigate building levels, floors, basements, and negative figures.
nodes.cheatsheet: Floors use ordinal numbers: **pierwsze piętro** (1st floor). Ground floor is **parter**. Basements use **piwnica** or **podziemie**. Negative temperatures/numbers use **minus** (e.g., *minus pięć*).

nodes.label: Buildings & Prepositions
nodes.description: Identify common spatial structures and describe basic physical layouts.
nodes.cheatsheet: Key structures: **budynek** (building), **dworzec** (station). Prepositions require case shifts: **na** (+Locative / on/at), **w** (+Locative / in), **pod** (+Instrumental / under), **za** (+Instrumental / behind).

nodes.label: Existence & Amount of People
nodes.description: Express presence using verbs of existence and count human groups.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use **jest** (there is) and **są** (there are). Negation switches to genitive **nie ma**. Counting people triggers special virile numerals: **dwaj mężczyźni** (2 men) or collective **dwie osoby** (2 people).

nodes.label: Relative Time: Day, Week, Month, Year
nodes.description: Anchor timelines cleanly across relative days, weeks, months, and years.
nodes.cheatsheet:
Timeline grid:
Days: **przedwczoraj** → **wczoraj** → **dzisiaj** → **jutro** → **pojutrze**.
Weeks: **zeszły tydzień** → **ten tydzień** → **przyszły tydzień**.
Months: **w zeszłym miesiącu** → **w tym miesiącu** → **w przyszłym miesiącu**.
Years: **w zeszłym roku** → **w tym roku** → **w przyszłym roku**.

nodes.label: Specific Time: This Morning & Next Week
nodes.description: Isolate immediate parts of a day or adjacent calendar blocks.
nodes.cheatsheet: Say 'this morning' using **dziś rano**. 'Next week' is **w przyszłym tygodniu** (uses locative case prepositional structure).

nodes.label: Months & Days of the Month
nodes.description: Identify calendar months and navigate ordinal days of the month.
nodes.cheatsheet: Months: **styczeń** (Jan), **luty** (Feb), etc. Dates use ordinal numbers in genitive case: 'the 1st of May' is **pierwszego maja**; 'the 4th' is **czwartego**.

nodes.label: Year & Month Durations
nodes.description: Calculate spans and durations of time using years and month blocks.
nodes.cheatsheet: Durations of months use **miesiąc** (1), **miesiące** (2-4), **miesięcy** (5+). Years use **rok** (1), **lata** (2-4), **lat** (5+). E.g., *przez dwa lata* (for 2 years).

nodes.label: Asking 'When' (Kiedy)
nodes.description: Formulate open-ended temporal questions about future, present, or past events.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use **Kiedy?** (*When?*). Example: **Kiedy jedziesz do Polski?** (*When are you going to Poland?*).

nodes.label: Meals & Food Types
nodes.description: Categorize daily meals and basic cultural food selections.
nodes.cheatsheet: Meals: **śniadanie** (breakfast), **obiad** (lunch/dinner), **kolacja** (supper). Traditional item example: **sushi** or **pierogi**.

nodes.label: Consumptive Actions (Eat & Drink)
nodes.description: Utilize active consumption roots for eating and drinking.
nodes.cheatsheet: Verbs: **jeść** (to eat) and **pić** (to drink). Direct objects following positive verbs take the Accusative case: **Jem sushi** (Accusative).

nodes.label: Verb Conjugation & Tenses
nodes.description: Conjugate verbs into affirmative, negative, past, and conditional desire states.
nodes.cheatsheet:
Using **jeść** (to eat):
Affirmative: **Jem** (*I eat*)
Negative: Switches object to Genitive! **Nie jem sushi** (*I don't eat sushi*)
Past: **Jadłem** (m) / **Jadłam** (f) (*I ate*)
Desire: **Chcę jeść** (*I want to eat*). Question: **Chcesz jeść?** (*Do you want to eat?*).

nodes.label: Conjunctions (Oraz & Potem)
nodes.description: Link phrases together or order successive events smoothly.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use **A także / Oraz** (*And also / Plus*) to add items to a list. Use **Potem / Następnie** (*And then / After that*) to sequence actions chronically.

nodes.label: Frequency of Verbs
nodes.description: Quantify how often routines occur using positive and negative adverbs.
nodes.cheatsheet: Adverbs: **często** (often), **czasami** (sometimes), **rzadko** (rarely). Negative: **nigdy** (never) *requires a double negative structure*—e.g., **Nigdy nie jem** (*I never eat*).

nodes.label: Transportation & Commuting
nodes.description: Describe modes of travel and ask others how they commute.
nodes.cheatsheet: Ask 'How do you commute?' using **Jak jedziesz do pracy?**. Use **samolotem** (by plane), **samochodem** (by car), **statkiem** (by boat) — all in the Instrumental case. Walking is **pieszo** or **idę na piechotę**.

nodes.label: Sports & Complex Preferences
nodes.description: Express likes regarding sports, distinguishing between what and what kind.
nodes.cheatsheet: Ask 'What sports do you like?' with **Co lubisz?** or 'What kind of sports...?' with **Jaki rodzaj sportu lubisz?**. Express preference via **Interesuję się** (+Instrumental) or **Lubię...** (+Accusative).
